summary: Sprint 2 full testing: Backend 17/17 tests passed. All frontend Sprint 2 features working after Metro bundle cache was cleared (cache had old pre-Sprint2 bundle). All features confirmed: Architect banner, High-Ticket badges, Architect Upgrade screen, Blueprint Guide, Stuck buttons, Architect Tools section, ArchitectPaywall modal.

minor.issue: Login response does not include `is_architect` field. Architect users who log out and log back in will have `is_architect=undefined` in AsyncStorage, causing them to see the 'Architect Tier Available' banner even though they're already subscribers. Fix: add `is_architect: user.get('is_architect', False)` to login and signup responses.
Non-architect history call during page load returns 403 which is caught silently - this is correct behavior but worth noting.

ui_bugs.issue: If user navigates directly to /blueprint-guide without ?idea_id=, API gets called with idea_id=undefined (omitted from JSON). Pydantic returns 422 before architect check, so UI shows 'Something went wrong' instead of 'Architect tier required'. Normal navigation flow always passes idea_id from idea-detail.tsx (line 445). Minor UX issue.

issues: Design looks clean and consistent with Sprint 1 dark theme. No contrast/spacing issues found.

action_items: CRITICAL: Add `is_architect` field to login (and signup) API responses in server.py. Currently `return {"id": user["id"], "email": user["email"], "name": user["name"], "is_guest": False, "profile": user.get("profile", {})}` — add `"is_architect": user.get("is_architect", False)`. Without this, architect users see the upgrade banner after re-login.
LOW: Remove unused `get_architect_status()` helper function at line 971 of server.py — it is defined but never called.
LOW: Consider modularizing server.py (1237 lines) into separate modules for maintainability.
LOW: Handle missing idea_id in blueprint-guide.tsx more gracefully — if ideaId is undefined, show a message asking user to start from an idea page instead of calling the API with undefined.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: Login/signup endpoints do not return `is_architect` field — architect users lose their status on re-login (AsyncStorage overwritten with data lacking is_architect). Fix in server.py login endpoint (line ~746): add `is_architect: user.get('is_architect', False)` to return dict.
DEAD CODE: `get_architect_status()` helper defined at line 971 is never used — subscription endpoint uses `user.get('is_architect', False)` directly.

IMPORTANT: Metro bundle had stale cache during initial testing. Sprint 2 code was not bundled. Required cache clear + server restart. This may indicate Metro cache invalidation issue in CI/deployment workflow.
LOW: Blueprint Guide 422 vs 403 when navigating without idea_id context
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing tester account which already has digital-001 blueprint started.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 2 backend and frontend tested and working. IMPORTANT: Metro bundle cache was stale and needed to be cleared (`rm -rf /app/frontend/.metro-cache/cache && sudo supervisorctl restart expo`). If you see Sprint 2 features missing from the UI, try this fix. Main outstanding issue: login API doesn't return is_architect, so architect users will see the upgrade banner after re-login. Test user: tester@blueprint.com / TestPass123 has digital-001 (Freelance Web Designer) started at 14% progress. To test Architect-only features you need a user with is_architect=true in MongoDB (or use the DB directly to set it).
rca_of_issue: Metro bundle cache issue: The Expo Metro bundler had a stale cache from before Sprint 2 code was added. The /app/frontend/.metro-cache/cache directory (81MB) contained old compiled module files. When Metro served the bundle, it used cached versions that didn't include the Sprint 2 code (architectBanner, is_architect checks, etc.). Fix: Clear cache dir and restart. The login API missing is_architect: Home screen condition `!user?.is_architect` evaluates to `!undefined = true` for architect users who re-logged in, incorrectly showing the upgrade banner. The login endpoint explicitly constructs the return dict without is_architect: `return {"id": user["id"], "email": user["email"], "name": user["name"], "is_guest": False, "profile": user.get("profile", {})}` on line ~746.
